proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 32 features, several binary indicators (feature_005, feature_008, feature_011, feature_013, feature_023) and continuous variables (feature_022, feature_020) are associated with the binary outcome objective_response, and at least one of the binary indicators is a treatment exposure with a non-trivial main effect.

analyses.code: for c in feats: chi2 or t-test of c vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Univariate screen: feature_013 (ORR 0.346 vs 0.154, chi2 p<1e-300), feature_008 (0.159 vs 0.361, chi2 p<1e-300), feature_022 continuous (responders mean 28.2 vs nonresponders 47.2, MW p=2e-252), feature_015 (0.260 vs 0.160), feature_001 ordinal (0.282/0.228/0.182), feature_021 (0.250 vs 0.150), feature_020 (3.50 vs 3.95), feature_027 (0.242 vs 0.176), feature_024 (5.69 vs 6.23), feature_002 (3.82 vs 3.80) are all p<0.001. feature_005, feature_011, feature_023, feature_006, feature_004, feature_017, feature_019, feature_010, age-like feature_016, and most labs (feature_007/014/032/026/etc.) have no main-effect signal (p>0.1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment for all features in a logistic regression, feature_008 = 1 is independently associated with HIGHER odds of objective_response, while feature_013 = 1, feature_015 = 1, feature_021 = 1, feature_027 = 1, higher feature_022, higher feature_020, higher feature_024, and higher feature_001 ordinal level are independently associated with LOWER odds of response.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic (n=50000, pseudo-R^2=0.124). Significant adjusted log-odds: feature_008 +1.201 (z=52.6), feature_013 -1.126 (z=-45.4), feature_015 -0.694 (z=-22.1), feature_021 -0.735 (z=-16.9), feature_027 -0.431 (z=-6.0), feature_001 -0.328 (z=-19.3), feature_022 (z) -0.097 (z=-5.5), feature_020 -0.140 (z=-12.1), feature_024 -0.068 (z=-5.4), feature_002 +0.064 (z=+5.6). All other features are not significant (|z|<1.8). feature_008 is the only candidate treatment-direction (positive) variable with a large effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 = 1 acts like a beneficial treatment indicator (it has the largest positive adjusted coefficient on objective_response), and its absolute effect is heterogeneous across patient subgroups defined by other features rather than uniform.

analyses.code: for each feature m: Logit(y ~ tx + m + tx*m); record p_value of tx:m
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-feature interaction screen with feature_008 as putative tx: feature_013 interaction beta=-1.978 (p<1e-300), feature_015 -1.237 (p=1.4e-93), feature_021 -1.138 (p=3.3e-42), feature_022 (z, log-scale) -0.805 (p=1.2e-72), feature_027 -1.283 (p=1.8e-19), feature_001 +0.141 (p=1.6e-5). Each of the four binary modifiers (feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027) attenuates the feature_008 effect by approximately the full size of the unmodified effect. Heterogeneity is overwhelming.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with feature_013 = 0, feature_008 = 1 is associated with a much higher objective_response rate than feature_008 = 0; in patients with feature_013 = 1 the effect of feature_008 is essentially null (zero or near-zero absolute difference).

analyses.code: for lvl in feature_013: chi2 of feature_008 x objective_response within stratum
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=0 (n=22519): ORR_on_feature_008=0.610, ORR_off=0.169, absolute diff=+0.440 (chi2 p<1e-300). feature_013=1 (n=27481): ORR_on=0.158, ORR_off=0.150, diff=+0.008 (chi2 p=0.087, NS). Treatment effect entirely concentrated in feature_013=0 stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with feature_015 = 0, feature_008 = 1 raises objective_response substantially; in patients with feature_015 = 1, feature_008 has no detectable effect.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_015; chi2 within each stratum

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with feature_021 = 0, feature_008 = 1 raises objective_response substantially; in patients with feature_021 = 1, feature_008 has no detectable effect.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_021; chi2 within stratum

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with feature_027 = 0, feature_008 = 1 raises objective_response substantially; in patients with feature_027 = 1 (a small ~3% subgroup), the effect of feature_008 is null or slightly negative.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_027; chi2 within stratum

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 (continuous, right-skewed, likely a tumor-burden / PSA-like marker) modifies the feature_008 treatment effect on the logit scale, with larger logit-scale effect in patients with lower feature_022.

However, on the absolute response-rate scale, within patients who already have all four binary modifiers favorable (feature_013=0, feature_015=0, feature_021=0, feature_027=0), the absolute treatment effect of feature_008 is approximately constant across feature_022 quartiles, so feature_022 is NOT an independent treatment-effect modifier beyond the four binary ones.

analyses.result_summary: Logit-scale interaction tx * log(feature_022) coef = -0.805 (p=1.2e-72). Higher feature_022 reduces the log-odds treatment effect.

analyses.code: within favorable subgroup, stratify by feature_022 quartile and compute ORR diff
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_013=0&feature_015=0&feature_021=0&feature_027=0 (n=15681): Q1 feature_022 [0.14,3.80] diff=+0.633; Q2 [3.81,7.40] +0.628; Q3 [7.41,14.44] +0.624; Q4 [14.45,356.76] +0.618. Confirmatory logit on this subset: tx*log(feature_022) coef = -0.041 (p=0.32, NS). Absolute effect is essentially flat once the four binary modifiers are favorable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (ordinal, levels 0/1/2 with prevalence 35%/50%/15%) is a prognostic factor inversely related to objective_response (higher level = lower response) but not a strong feature_008 treatment-effect modifier on the absolute scale within the favorable subgroup.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_001 within favorable subgroup; also tx*feature_001 in full model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effect of feature_001 in the multivariable logit: -0.328 per level (p<1e-80). Within favorable subgroup: feature_001=0 ORR_on=0.859 vs ORR_off=0.213, diff=+0.646; feature_001=1 0.781 vs 0.159, diff=+0.622; feature_001=2 0.709 vs 0.119, diff=+0.590. Absolute treatment effect is mildly attenuated at higher feature_001 (-3 to -6 pp per level) but remains huge.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features that have NO main effect on objective_response (feature_005, feature_011, feature_023, feature_006, feature_004, feature_017, feature_019), none is a hidden treatment with strong heterogeneity — i.e., none has any subgroup interaction with another feature where the interaction coefficient |beta|>0.3 and p<1e-6.

analyses.code: for each pair of binary features (tx, mod) with no overlap with feature_008/013/015/021/027: Logit(y ~ tx + mod + tx*mod); collect interactions with p<1e-6 and |beta|>0.3
analyses.result_summary: Comprehensive pairwise binary-feature interaction scan returned strong hits ONLY among the set {feature_008, feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027}. No null-main-effect candidate (feature_005, feature_011, feature_023, feature_006, feature_004, feature_017, feature_019) showed any interaction passing the |beta|>0.3 and p<1e-6 threshold. The dataset contains exactly one strong treatment-like binary (feature_008) with heterogeneity confined to the four bad-marker modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The number of bad modifiers a patient carries (feature_013 + feature_015 + feature_021 + feature_027) acts as a switch: with zero bad modifiers the feature_008 effect is large (~+63 pp absolute), and with one or more bad modifiers the effect collapses to ~0 pp — i.e., the relationship is closer to step-function than dose-response.

analyses.code: df['bad']=df[mods].sum(1); for k in 0..4: ORR_on - ORR_off, chi2

proposed_hypotheses.text: All four bad modifiers contribute non-redundantly to defining the responsive subgroup: dropping any one of them from the joint definition decreases the within-subgroup feature_008 effect noticeably (most for feature_013, then feature_015, then feature_021, then feature_027).

analyses.code: for drop in mods: subset = (other 3 mods all 0); compute ORR_on - ORR_off
analyses.result_summary: Drop feature_013 (subset n=34844): diff=+0.287. Drop feature_015 (n=19621): +0.505. Drop feature_021 (n=17456): +0.563. Drop feature_027 (n=16175): +0.607. feature_013 is the strongest individual switch; feature_027 contributes least but still adds discriminatory power.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for objective_response: feature_008 = 1 (vs feature_008 = 0) increases the probability of objective_response, and the effect is concentrated in the subgroup defined by feature_013 = 0 AND feature_015 = 0 AND feature_021 = 0 AND feature_027 = 0. Within that subgroup the absolute response rate rises from ~17% (off) to ~80% (on), an absolute increase of ~63 percentage points; outside that subgroup the absolute effect is ~0.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ tx + fav + tx*fav + feature_001 + log(feature_022) + feature_020) where fav = (feature_013==0)&(feature_015==0)&(feature_021==0)&(feature_027==0)
analyses.result_summary: Pseudo-R^2=0.202. tx (in fav=0): coef +0.061 (p=0.045), OR=1.06. tx_fav: +2.939 (p<1e-300). Combined within-favorable tx coef = +3.00, OR ~ 20.1. Within-favorable absolute ORR: 0.798 vs 0.172 (diff=+0.626). Outside favorable absolute ORR: 0.161 vs 0.153 (diff=+0.008). Subgroup hypothesis is overwhelmingly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other prognostic features (feature_020, feature_024, feature_002) are independent prognostic factors for objective_response but are NOT treatment-effect modifiers for feature_008 once the four-binary subgroup is conditioned on (their tx interaction p-values exceed 0.05 in the favorable-subgroup-restricted model).

analyses.code: within favorable: Logit(y ~ tx + cont + tx*cont) for each continuous feature
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 (log, z): tx_mod coef -0.041 p=0.32. feature_020: tx_mod p>0.2. feature_024: tx_mod p>0.1. feature_002: tx_mod p>0.05. None of these continuous variables modify the feature_008 effect on the absolute scale once the four-binary subgroup is fixed; they remain prognostic main effects.
